mnemonics: T.H.R.E.S.H.: T - Turn grayscale, H - Histogram analysis, R - Reference value (T), E - Evaluate pixels, S - Separate objects, H - Harden boundaries
keyPoints: Thresholding converts a grayscale image to a binary image using a fixed value T
Pixels with intensity greater than T become white (1), others become black (0)
Global thresholding uses a single threshold value for the entire image
commonMistakes: Assuming a single global threshold works well for all images (fails with uneven lighting)
Choosing threshold value arbitrarily instead of using histogram analysis methods
Confusing thresholding with segmentation (thresholding is a basic segmentation technique)
